cover.title: THE OCEAN
cover.subtitle: The one who opens the surface easily, while keeping the deepest room locked.
cover.tagline: You can get close to anyone, but depth is the door you rarely open.

profile_scores.caption: High drive and stability, low competitiveness and pride. You get close to anyone easily, but the deepest part stays locked separately.

short_summary: You can get close quickly, warmly, and naturally, but that doesn't mean you've let someone all the way in.
Your loneliness doesn't come from having too few people. It comes from keeping the deepest room untouched.
What changes love for you is not opening wider, but opening one place deeply.

full_report.headline: THE OCEAN
full_report.intro: You're someone who makes friends easily. No matter who you meet, conversation flows and people get comfortable fast. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why surface closeness happens quickly for you, why the deepest room stays locked, and how one relationship can finally move beyond the surface.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You usually make friends easily.
Conversation often flows, and people can get comfortable with you quickly.
People may look at you and think, "this person gets along with everyone."
But getting along with someone and letting them into your deepest inner world are different things for you.
You may not think of that as hiding. It can simply feel like a separate part of yourself.
You can stay close with many people while only a few truly know what matters most inside you.
It's the same when you love.
At first, you click, you're comfortable, and you get close fast.
When the relationship starts moving deeper, you may still look open and comfortable while keeping the most private part untouched.
chapters.profile_note: High Drive · High Stability, Low Competitiveness · Low Pride. You get close to anyone easily, but the deepest part stays locked separately.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat: THE SURFACE OCEAN CURVE
body: Early in a relationship, you may become comfortable with someone faster than they expect.
Conversation flows, the vibe feels natural, the other person gets comfortable fast.
The difficulty can begin after that.
As the relationship deepens, it may look as if you are showing more, while one important part still stays out of reach.
The other person may assume that closeness means they now know the deeper parts of you too.
But you may keep stopping just before that door opens.
The surface can keep growing warmer while the distance to your core changes very little.
chapters.highlight: The loneliness is not always about having too few people. It can come from having no place where the deepest part of you is shared.

chart.title: THE SURFACE OCEAN CURVE
chart.description: The pattern where the surface gets close fast early on, but even as the relationship goes on, the distance to the core never shrinks, and only the surface intimacy keeps widening.

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: You may get along with people easily. But ease and depth are not always the same thing.
items.truth_short: Getting along well can feel close without becoming truly deep.

items.truth: You may not feel hidden. You may simply think some parts are private. But the other person can still sense when a deeper door has not opened.
items.truth_short: You may not feel hidden, but the deepest part can still remain closed.

items.belief: I meet so many people, so I'm not lonely.
items.truth: Meeting many people can bring warmth and energy. It does not always remove the loneliness that comes from missing depth.
items.truth_short: People can be everywhere, while deeper closeness is still missing.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: Different people can create the same kind of distance.
You may feel comfortable with someone who does not ask much about what lies underneath.
Someone satisfied with the current level of closeness. Someone who does not press for more.
That can feel easy, so the relationship may stay at that exact depth for a long time.
chapters.highlight: Someone who enjoys your company sees your openness. Someone who wants real closeness stays curious about what you rarely show.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need is not more people around you.
You already know how to create connection.
What may help is choosing one person and opening one private part a little more.
You do not have to show everything.
One honest thing you have rarely said may be enough to begin.
For you, that small opening can be a meaningful step toward depth.
chapters.action_sentence: Depth can begin with one honest part of you being shared with one person.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You already know how to be comfortable with many kinds of people.
That is a real strength.
What you may learn next is how to move past ease and into depth with one person.
Not opening to everyone. Opening one place more fully.
That may feel more vulnerable than broad connection ever did.
But the ability to create connection is already there. This is a different way of using it.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time learning how to connect widely. The next step is learning how to stay with depth in one relationship.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: Someone Who Does Not Ask What Lies Underneath
items.description: They enjoy your company exactly as it is. No difficult questions. No pressure. Months can pass comfortably while very little of your inner world is actually shared.

items.type_title: Someone Who Loves Conversation but Avoids Personal Questions
items.description: You can talk for hours about stories, jokes, and ideas. But when the conversation turns personal, it may move away again before anything deeper is said.
They tell you a great deal early on. Family stories, old heartbreaks, future plans. Because they feel open, they may assume you have shared just as much.

items.type_title: Someone Who Mistakes Frequency for Depth
items.description: You meet often and talk every day. That can feel intimate. But frequency alone does not always mean the most important parts have been seen.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Some of these signs may show up early. Warmth on the surface can make them easy to overlook.

signal_strength.label: You get close fast, but barely have any deep conversations
You may start treating surface closeness as the natural limit of the relationship
The relationship has lasted a while, but doesn't feel much deeper
The other person may not notice the distance because the surface already feels warm

chapters.core_signal: The other person may feel they know you well, while an important part of you still has not entered the relationship.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: They wait without demanding depth.
checklist.description: They do not rush you, but they stay open to knowing more when you are ready.

checklist.title: They tell the difference between surface and real intimacy.
checklist.description: They can tell the difference between being socially close and being emotionally known.

checklist.title: They don't find it heavy when you show your core.
checklist.description: When you share something private, they can receive it without dismissing it or making it feel too heavy.

checklist.title: Being together makes things deeper.
checklist.description: The relationship keeps making room for more honesty, not only more time together.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone who is satisfied with surface closeness and never asks for more. The relationship can feel warm and easy while the deeper distance stays untouched. Over time, that comfort may begin to feel lonely.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone who notices when the conversation stays on the surface, but does not force you past your pace. They show curiosity, patience, and care when you share something more private.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone who enjoys your openness but never asks what lies underneath. That does not mean the same pattern has to continue. What changes it is whether the relationship keeps making room for real depth, not only easy connection.
sections.next_signs: This time, the pattern may show up in a brief pause before a deeper answer. You may feel the urge to change the subject or turn the moment into a joke. That pause can be a chance to choose whether you want to stay on the surface or share a little more.
sections.try_this: When a deeper question comes up, stay with it for one more sentence. Share one honest thing without turning it into a full confession. Then notice whether the relationship continues to feel respectful, curious, and safe over time.
sections.final_line: One answer will not settle it. What matters is whether depth keeps finding a place between you.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to create wide connection. What's left is learning how to open one place deeply.
action_items: This week, tell one trusted person something you usually keep private.
Notice when you change the subject just before a deeper answer.
Choose one relationship where emotional depth matters more than how often you talk.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to be comfortable with anyone. This report covered why you've always stopped at the surface, and how you can learn to go deeper with one person.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
